semanticCore: Pen, reed-cutting, arrow for casting lots, clipping, or trim
quranicRelevance: Writing pen (Qalam - 'noon wal-qalami', 'allama bil-qalami'), and casting arrows/lots ('aqla-mahum').

keyDerivations.meaning: Pen, writing instrument, reed-pen, or lot-casting arrow

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَا كُنْتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يُلْقُونَ أَقْلَامَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ يَكْفُلُ مَرْيَمَ (3:44): And you were not with them when they cast their pens/arrows (to decide) which of them should take charge of Mary.
discardedNoise: Nail clippers (Meqlam) in modern hygiene bags.
summaryEn: The root represents pens and arrows (Qalam), honoring the pen as the tool of divine teaching and detailing the temple priests casting arrows to care for Mary.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر القلم وأعواد السهام المقصوصة، مشيداً بالقلم كأداة تعليم الوحي (والقلم)، وواصفاً إلقاء سهام الاقتراع لكفالة مريم (أقلامهم).
rawText: قَلَمٌ and 🡻قَلَمَانِ and 🡻قَلَمَانٌ: see جَلَمٌ. === A writing-reed prepared for writing; a reed-pen. قَلَمَانٌ : see قَلمٌ. مُقَلَّمُ الظُّفْرِ and الأَظْفَارِ: see ظُفرٌ.
